page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX-512 instructions. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.se1.2xlarge exhibits modest compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths perform at average levels, reaching 39.75 GB/sec and 36.51 GB/sec respectively. The instance demonstrates average throughput for Redis SET operations and small-model LLM inference, but struggles with more intensive tasks, showing poor performance in single-threaded compression and medium-model LLM text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1.2xlarge offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-frequency CPU performance. Because it lacks local storage, it relies on network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is best suited for workloads such as in-memory caching, Redis databases, lightweight database operations, and general-purpose memory-intensive applications that can operate within a 2 Gbps network baseline.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities, alongside average large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET throughput. Qualitatively, this instance provides a cost-effective allocation of memory resources for workloads that are not CPU-intensive. It is best suited for in-memory caching, Redis deployments, and memory-bound applications requiring moderate network throughput.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, ideal for in-memory caching and database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound applications and in-memory database caching.
